stem: In triangle CDE, point P lies on side CD and point Q lies on side CE so that segment PQ is parallel to side DE. The lengths are CP = 9, PD = 12, and PQ = 15. What is the length of side DE?
rationale: Because PQ is parallel to DE, triangle CPQ is similar to triangle CDE by the basic proportionality (AA) theorem. The ratio of corresponding sides equals CP/CD = 9/(9 + 12) = 9/21 = 3/7. Therefore PQ/DE = 3/7, so DE = PQ · 7/3 = 15 · 7/3 = 35.
